request.system:
You are a regulatory analyst. Given a prediction-market contract and a single regulatory news record, decide if this record is relevant to whether the contract resolves YES or NO. Score relevance 0-10 (0 = off-topic, 10 = directly determinative). Return JSON matching the schema. one_line_why should be ≤160 chars and explain how this record moves resolution probability.

Direction: judge whether this event makes the contract MORE likely to resolve YES (= bullish), LESS likely to resolve YES (= bearish), or has no directional effect (= neutral). IMPORTANT: direction is always relative to YES resolution. If your one_line_why says the event 'increases odds' or 'raises probability', direction MUST be bullish. If it says 'decreases odds' or 'lowers probability', direction MUST be bearish. Do not contradict yourself.

Magnitude: high = materially changes probability, medium = notable but not decisive, low = incremental signal.

Timeline shift: does this event suggest resolution will come sooner or later than expected, or no change (none)?
request.user:
Contract title: Will the US enter a recession in 2026?
Resolution criteria: Resolves YES if the National Bureau of Economic Research (NBER) Business Cycle Dating Committee announces that the United States entered an economic recession with a peak month in 2026, on or before 2026-12-31. Resolves NO otherwise.
Conditions:
  A: NBER 2026-peak recession by 2026-12-31 — The NBER Business Cycle Dating Committee publicly announces that the U.S. entered a recession with the peak month occurring in 2026, and this announcement is made on or before 2026-12-31.
Settlement entities: ['National Bureau of Economic Research', 'Federal Reserve System', 'Federal Open Market Committee', 'Department of the Treasury', 'Bureau of Labor Statistics', 'Bureau of Economic Analysis', 'Council of Economic Advisers']

Record title: Treasury Sanctions Cartel-Linked Casinos and Key Associates on U.S.-Mexico Border
Record date: 2026-04-14
Record regulator: U.S. Department of the Treasury Office of Foreign Assets Control
Record topic: United States Department of the Treasury
Record entities: ['Abdon Federico Rodriguez Garcia', 'Comercializadora y Arrendadora de Mexico, S.A. de C.V.', 'Department of State', 'Drug Enforcement Administration', 'Eduardo Javier Islas Valdez', 'Jesus Reymundo Ramos Vazquez', 'Juan Pablo Penilla Rodriguez', 'Miguel Angel Trevino Morales', 'Office of Foreign Assets Control', 'Scott Bessent', 'U.S. Department of the Treasury']

response.one_line_why: OFAC sanctions on Mexico-linked casinos are unrelated to broad US macro conditions and unlikely to affect NBER's 2026 recession determination.
